    11	// workingDirKey is the context key used to thread a per-request WorkingDir
    12	// through GraphQL resolvers. LAYER 2 of the GraphQL multi-project fix
    13	// (ddx-055e8d32) lets the scoped /api/projects/{project}/graphql route inject
    14	// the resolved project's WorkingDir into the request context so the
    15	// singleton resolver constructed at server start can serve any project
    16	// without per-request reconstruction.
    17	type workingDirKey struct{}
    18	
    19	// WithWorkingDir returns ctx with workingDir attached for downstream resolver
    20	// access. The server's GraphQL HTTP handler MUST call this before delegating
    21	// to the gqlgen handler so resolvers read the request-scoped project root
    22	// rather than the resolver struct's fallback default.
    23	func WithWorkingDir(ctx context.Context, workingDir string) context.Context {
    24		if workingDir == "" {
    25			return ctx
    26		}
    27		return context.WithValue(ctx, workingDirKey{}, workingDir)
    28	}
    29	
    30	// WorkingDirFromContext returns the WorkingDir previously attached via
    31	// WithWorkingDir, or the empty string if none was set. Callers should prefer
    32	// (*Resolver).workingDir(ctx) which falls back to the resolver's default.
    33	func WorkingDirFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
    34		if ctx == nil {
    35			return ""
    36		}
    37		if v, ok := ctx.Value(workingDirKey{}).(string); ok {
    38			return v
    39		}
    40		return ""
    41	}
    42	
    43	// workingDir returns the request-scoped WorkingDir (via WithWorkingDir) when
    44	// present, falling back to r.WorkingDir. All resolver methods with access to
    45	// ctx should call this rather than reading r.WorkingDir directly so the same
    46	// resolver instance can serve multiple projects safely.
    47	func (r *Resolver) workingDir(ctx context.Context) string {
    48		if dir := WorkingDirFromContext(ctx); dir != "" {
    49			return dir
    50		}
    51		return r.WorkingDir
    52	}

ddx work currently continues scanning and claiming ready beads after setup/infrastructure failures. During the May 6 ENOSPC incident, ddx work failed to create one isolated worktree, then immediately claimed another production-reachability bead and failed again. Resource exhaustion must stop the drain because continuing expands the blast radius.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 initializes loop events and exitReason but does not include resource_exhausted as a recognized loop-fatal exit reason.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:529 onward continues iterating after non-success reports unless existing retry/no-progress/once logic stops it; there is no host-resource circuit breaker.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:263-283 defines status taxonomy around execution_failed/no_evidence/etc., but there is no distinct resource_exhausted status/reason for loop policy to inspect.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:434-440 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:281-284 require ddx work to stop claiming new beads after resource_exhausted.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable resource_exhausted attempt/loop classification that can be returned by ddx try/resource preflight and recognized by ExecuteBeadWorker.
- On resource_exhausted, release any non-terminal claim, emit cleanup/resource events, set loop.end exit_reason=resource_exhausted, and return without scanning the next ready bead.
- Print an operator-visible message such as resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop.
- Ensure resource_exhausted is not counted as model no-progress, capability retry, or review/quota retry.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the resource checker itself here; this bead consumes the outcome from the try/resource-preflight bead.
- Do not change queue ranking or production-reachability bead priority.
- Do not change provider 429 in-attempt retry behavior.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop creates two ready beads and an executor returning resource_exhausted for the first; verifies the second bead is never claimed or executed.
